title: Weser
content:
The Weser () is a river of Lower Saxony in north-west Germany. It begins at Hannoversch Münden through the confluence of the Werra and Fulda. It passes through the Hanseatic city of Bremen. Its mouth is further north against the ports of Bremerhaven and Nordenham. The latter is on the Butjadingen Peninsula. It then merges into the North Sea via two highly saline, estuarine mouths. It connects to the canal network running east-west across the North German Plain.

The river combined with the Werra – a dialectal form of Weser – runs. This makes up the longest river wholly in Germany. The Weser itself is long. The Werra rises in Thuringia, the German State south of the main projection (tongue) of Lower Saxony. Etymology. The Weser and the Werra were the same words in different dialects. The difference reflects the old linguistic border between Central and Low German, passing through Hannoversch Münden.
